title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims · Runtime Recall

Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for static resource and delivery evidence dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.
Separate wire, decoded, retained, intrinsic-media, rendered, and duplicate-transfer sizes with missing evidence visible.
Report cache fields, validators, variation, redirects, compression, and cold or warm assumptions without simulating browser cache blindly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Resource Reference Graph · changed case · Review Resource Reference…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 7515 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Go team handles case 6801 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: net/http duration measures complete user-perceived performance.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims. Artifact under review: static resource and delivery evidence dossier.

options.text: The Performance Nonclaim record says static resource and delivery evidence dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Performance Nonclaim.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Performance Nonclaim for static resource and delivery evidence dossier.
The record names the assumption, applies Performance Nonclaim, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Performance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Performance Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 1944 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Performance Nonclaim.
steps.why: Retrieving Performance Nonclaim through the concrete static resource and delivery evidence dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Performance Nonclaim, keep Performance Nonclaim connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Performance Nonclaim, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Performance Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Performance Nonclaim, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 1944 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Performance Nonclaim, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: net/http duration measures complete user-perceived performance.
